title: albizia fastigiata
intro: Albizia fastigiata, also known as the East Indian walnut or Indian walnut, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The bark and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, boasting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Albizia fastigiata have been found to help in treating various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, while its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of Albizia fastigiata are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
